sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system data documented for this NES shmup

special_mechanics.name: Ship Upgrade/Wing System
special_mechanics.description: Player can acquire 'wing' upgrades that transform the ship into a larger variant, allowing the player to sustain two enemy hits instead of one and enabling weapon upgrades beyond standard ship limitations

special_mechanics.name: Weapon System
special_mechanics.description: Five primary weapons with unlimited ammunition (standard gun, flamethrower, guided boomerang, etc.). Weapons obtained via numbered circles and upgraded progressively up to 5 levels by collecting matching circles. Upgrades change weapon behavior and power

special_mechanics.name: Bomb System
special_mechanics.description: Four types of secondary bombs (F, T, B, W variants) obtained via lettered circles. Limited ammunition. Upgraded versions become twice as powerful but consume twice as much ammunition

special_mechanics.name: Between-Level Shop
special_mechanics.description: Money collected during levels can be used to purchase upgrades between stages

tier_notes: Tier 2 - Qualitative data only. Gun-Nac is a scrolling shmup with no documented precise movement physics parameters. Sources focus on gameplay mechanics, story, regional differences, and development notes rather than technical movement specifications. The game features a stationary/floating player ship with weapon and bomb upgrade systems rather than character platforming physics.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Gun-Nac article
